name: The Chocolate Dome
recipeIngredient: 1 cup orange juice, sweetened to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a pastry brush, butter and flour an 8-inch bowl for baking the cake.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
3. Beat the eggs and sugar until thick and fluffy, tripling in volume.
4. Quickly sift and fold in the flour.
5. Pour batter into prepared bowl and bake for 35-45 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.
6. Cool cake completely on a wire rack (about 1 hour) before decorating.
7. Melt 5 ounces of chocolate over hot, not boiling, water in a double boiler. Let cool to room temperature.
8. Whip cream to soft peaks in a bowl set over ice cubes and water. Fold half the cream into the chocolate; fold in the rest of the cream.
9. Add Grand Marnier to orange juice mixture.
10. Slice Genoise horizontally into three layers of equal thickness. Discard crusty top.
11. Line an 8-inch bowl with plastic wrap. Fit rounded top onto the first layer, soaking with orange juice mixture. Spoon mousse and raspberries onto the layer.
12. Place second layer on first, brushing with remaining orange juice mixture, mousse, raspberries, and a slice of cake. Brush last of orange juice, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for several hours.
13. Melt remaining chocolate in double boiler.
14. Unmold chilled cake, domed side up, onto a rack.
15. Spread thin disk of chocolate on sheet of plastic wrap. Slide plastic into bowl, fold over it, gently drop chilled cake into bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and chill.
16. Make chocolate leaves from leftover chocolate.
17. Puree raspberries with sugar in blender or food processor. Add lemon juice. Push sauce through fine sieve.
18. Unmold cake and garnish with sauce and leaves.
